description_long: This object consists of three German stick grenades, known as 'Stielhandgranate', bundled together side by side. Each grenade features a cylindrical metal or Bakelite head attached to a long wooden handle, with one grenade displaying a tan color and a blue cap, while the others are olive green and dark gray with visible German text. The handles are wooden, showing signs of wear and a slightly rough texture, and the heads are metallic or composite, with one having a visible pull cord or fuse mechanism on top. The grenades are tightly grouped, suggesting a bundled configuration, which was historically used to increase explosive power. The visible text and markings on the heads indicate their military origin and provide handling instructions. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with a mix of painted and bare metal surfaces, and the object is compact enough to be carried by hand.
description: A bundle of three German stick grenades with cylindrical heads and wooden handles, featuring military markings and a mix of olive, tan, and gray colors.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three grenade heads, with visible German text and the blue cap on the tan grenade.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the side profiles of the grenades and the alignment of their handles.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the tops of the grenade heads, especially the tan grenade's blue cap and fuse.
description_view_3: Back view showing the handles and the less detailed sides of the grenade heads.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: Detonation is likely initiated by pulling a cord or unscrewing the fuse cap on the top of each grenade, which would trigger the internal explosive mechanism.
